title: PracticePanel
description: A confident, clinical-grade wordmark for a practice-management platform built for healthcare teams.
overview: PracticePanel needed a mark that could sit on a login screen, a business card, and a hospital dashboard without losing weight. The brief called for something that read as software-grade precision, not a generic medical cross.
problem: The founders had three prior logo attempts that all leaned on medical clichés — crosses, heartbeats, stethoscopes — and none of them felt like software.
solution: Designed a geometric wordmark with a custom-cut 'P' monogram that doubles as a UI tile, so the same mark scales from a 16px favicon to a signage-sized wall logo without redrawing.
process: Explored 14 monogram directions, tested legibility at favicon size first (mobile-in reverse), then built the full type system around the two survivors before client review.
outcome: Approved on the first formal round; the mark is now live across the product's web app, onboarding emails, and App Store listing.

title: Telecast: The Tawi Tales
description: A 6-second animated logo intro that opens every episode of a regional storytelling YouTube series.
overview: The Tawi Tales needed a title sequence that felt cinematic on a documentary budget — something viewers would recognise within the first second of a thumbnail autoplay.
problem: The channel's existing static logo disappeared in the YouTube feed and gave no sense of the show's tone before the host started talking.
solution: Built a short kinetic-type sequence with a paper-texture wipe and a river-motif line animation, referencing the Tawi river the series is named after.
process: Storyboarded the beat-by-beat motion in Illustrator, then animated and rendered in After Effects, timing the reveal to a 6-second broadcast-safe cut.
outcome: Now used as the channel's fixed cold-open across every episode, with viewers recognising the ident within the first watch-through.

title: MedSimplified Learning Series
description: A repeatable thumbnail and reel system for a medical-education channel, built for click-through, not decoration.
overview: MedSimplified publishes dense clinical topics for medical students. The visuals had to simplify without oversimplifying the science.
problem: Inconsistent thumbnails across uploads meant the channel looked like ten different creators, and click-through on new uploads was flat.
solution: Created a modular thumbnail template — fixed type hierarchy, consistent diagram style, single accent colour per subject — so any new upload could be built in minutes and still look like the same channel.
process: Audited 40+ existing uploads, identified the three best-performing layouts, then rebuilt them into a locked template with swappable diagram slots.
outcome: Rolled out across the channel's weekly upload schedule as the standing visual system for thumbnails and end-cards.

title: LifeMechanical Product Visuals
description: Catalogue-grade product visuals and print collateral for an international mechanical-parts brand.
overview: LifeMechanical sells precision components internationally and needed print-ready visuals that read as engineering-grade, not stock photography.
problem: Existing product sheets used raw manufacturer photos with no consistent lighting, framing, or brand type treatment.
solution: Built a standard product-shot layout with consistent lighting direction, callout system for specs, and a print-safe colour profile for offset runs.
process: Worked directly with the client's product photos, re-composited backgrounds and callouts, then proofed a CMYK-safe version before print.
outcome: Adopted as the standard format for the client's product catalogue and trade-show handouts.

title: Trade Academy Social Series
description: A modular social carousel system built to explain trading concepts in under 10 swipes.
overview: Trade Academy teaches retail trading concepts to a beginner audience across Instagram and LinkedIn.
problem: Educational carousels were text-heavy slabs that lost readers by slide three.
solution: Designed a repeatable carousel grid with a fixed number of words per slide, a single chart-style diagram per concept, and a consistent close-out CTA slide.
process: Reviewed engagement drop-off on past carousels slide-by-slide, then rebuilt the format around the slide where most readers left.
outcome: Completion rate on new carousels improved noticeably against the channel's prior format.

title: VectorGlobe Brand Refresh
description: A visual identity refresh for a global logistics-tech client moving from a legacy mark to a flatter, digital-first system.
overview: VectorGlobe's original mark was designed for print in 2011 and hadn't been adapted for app icons, dark UI, or motion.
problem: The legacy mark broke apart at small sizes and had no defined rules for use on dark backgrounds or in motion.
solution: Simplified the mark to a single-weight vector form, defined a two-colour system for light and dark contexts, and built a small motion-logo variant for app splash screens.
process: Audited every existing use of the old mark, then rebuilt from the smallest required size upward.
outcome: Rolled out across the client's app, site, and partner-facing documents as the current standard mark.

title: Seabird Education — Lead Generation Creatives
description: High-converting Meta ad creatives built for a monthly ad budget of ₹15–18 lakh, run for a K-12 admissions funnel.
overview: Seabird Education runs continuous admissions campaigns on Meta and needed a creative pipeline that could be tested, killed, and refreshed weekly without losing brand consistency.
problem: Ad fatigue was setting in fast on a fixed creative set, and cost-per-lead was climbing month over month.
solution: Built a rotating library of static and short-video ad creatives structured around 4 tested hooks, with a consistent brand frame so the ads still read as one campaign even as the creative churned.
process: Ran weekly creative testing cycles, tracked CTR and cost-per-lead per creative, and rebuilt the losing variants using the winning hook structure.
outcome: Sustained a healthy cost-per-lead across a ₹15–18 lakh monthly Meta ad budget while keeping creative fatigue in check through weekly refreshes.

title: GreenAppleActive Ad Creative Set
description: A full-funnel ad creative set for an international activewear brand, from cold-audience awareness through retargeting.
overview: GreenAppleActive needed distinct creative for three funnel stages rather than one ad set spread thin across a whole audience.
problem: A single generic ad set was being shown to cold and warm audiences alike, with no message tailored to where the viewer actually was in the funnel.
solution: Split creative into three tiers — awareness, consideration, retargeting — with a different message and format for each, sharing one consistent visual system.
process: Mapped the existing funnel, identified the message gap at each stage, then built creative sets per stage with clear CTAs matched to intent.
outcome: Delivered a structured, stage-matched creative library that replaced the client's single-ad-set approach.

title: Seabird Education — Admissions Landing Page
description: A single-purpose admissions landing page designed to pair with the Meta ad funnel and convert clicks into form submissions.
overview: The ad creatives were performing, but traffic was landing on a general school website with no clear next step.
problem: Ad click-through was healthy but form-fill conversion on the destination page was weak because visitors had too many competing links to choose from.
solution: Designed a single-scroll landing page with one form, one CTA repeated at anchor points, and messaging that mirrored the ad creative that sent the click.
process: Matched landing page headline and imagery directly to each top-performing ad variant so the click-to-landing experience felt continuous.
outcome: Improved form-submission rate against the previous general-website destination.

title: CBPA Event Poster Series
description: A poster series for a professional association's event calendar, built as one flexible template across the year.
overview: CBPA runs a recurring calendar of professional events and needed posters that felt consistent without looking repeated.
problem: Each event's poster was being designed from a blank page, costing time and producing an inconsistent visual identity across the calendar.
solution: Built one modular poster template with swappable colour, date, and speaker-photo slots so any new event could go from brief to print-ready in under an hour.
process: Standardised the type hierarchy and grid first, then tested the template against three very different event types to confirm it held up.
outcome: Adopted as the standing template for the association's event calendar.
